meta.label: Fattoush (Levantine Bread Salad)
meta.description: Bright, crunchy Levantine salad with toasted pita, crisp vegetables, fresh herbs, and a tangy sumac-lemon dressing.

summary: Fattoush is a classic Levantine salad built around crunchy vegetables, fresh herbs, and shards of toasted or fried pita. The signature flavor comes from sumac in the dressing (tangy, lemony) and a bright lemon-garlic vinaigrette. For best texture, keep the pita separate until serving so it stays crisp.

timing.notes: Toast pita while chopping vegetables to keep it fast.

compat.notes: Pita can be toasted in oven or crisped in a skillet.

foodSafetyNotes: Wash and dry greens and herbs thoroughly.
Dress salad just before serving for best texture.

ingredients.label: Pita bread
ingredients.notes: Split and cut into bite-size pieces.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes, chopped
ingredients.notes: About 2 large tomatoes. Use ripe, flavorful tomatoes.

ingredients.label: Pomegranate molasses (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp. Adds depth and sweet-tart balance.

steps.title: Toast the pita
instructions: Toss pita pieces with olive oil and a pinch of salt (optional).
Spread on a sheet pan and toast 8–12 minutes, stirring once, until crisp and golden.
Cool completely so it stays crunchy.

steps.title: Chop the vegetables and herbs
instructions: Chop romaine, cucumber, tomatoes, and any optional vegetables.
Keep vegetables cold/crisp while pita cools.

steps.title: Make the dressing
instructions: In a bowl or jar, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, sumac, grated garlic, pomegranate molasses (optional), salt, and black pepper.
Taste and adjust: more lemon for brightness, more salt for pop, more sumac for tang.

steps.title: Toss and serve
instructions: In a large bowl, combine vegetables and herbs.
Add dressing and toss to coat.
Add toasted pita right before serving (or serve pita on the side so it stays crisp longer).

notes: Texture tip: Add pita at the last moment. If making ahead, store pita separately in an airtight container.
Herb ratio: Parsley is essential; mint adds classic Levantine lift. Adjust to taste.
Flavor balance: Sumac provides the signature tang; pomegranate molasses adds sweet-tart depth but is optional.

variations.label: Fried pita (more traditional, richer)
changes: Instead of oven-toasting, shallow-fry pita pieces in 1/4 inch oil until golden.
Drain on paper towels and season lightly with salt.

variations.label: Add purslane (if available)
changes: Add 1 cup purslane leaves for an authentic, lemony bite.

variations.label: Extra sumac finish
changes: Sprinkle an additional 1/2 tsp sumac over the salad just before serving.
